prompt:
I have to apply for verification on Context7 and will give the site URL 'https://llms.jgwill.com/' and obviously it is outdated.

I am not seeking to have a README.md that points to all other resources rather in ./docs (which probably don't exist), not just context7 would fetch and visit that page to make credible my work within this repo.

For which in context7 I described as:
"Guillaume Descoteaux-Isabelle LLMS-txt for its 'Interdisciplinary portfolio at the nexus of Indigenous epistemologies, AI systems, and narrative storytelling'
I use it with my AI Companions to guide them with orientations.
This repository contains knowledge protected under the Indigenous Knowledge Stewardship License, which recognizes that knowledge belongs to the relationships, land, and communities that created it, not to individuals or corporations.
It also contains Robert Fritz's work."

Given that in GitHub Pages, we publish ./ and not ./docs, what is in the README.md should be minimal, point to ./docs which is where you will do most of your amazing work of discovering all that I have made available in here as part of my "Interdisciplinary portfolio at the nexus of Indigenous epistemologies, AI systems, and narrative storytelling."

This repo will evolve (like we (AI Companion like you and me) skills like .github/skills/ to start separating and making this more obvious for LLM and human to access them (which probably relate to things like llms-rise-framework.txt, llms-creative-orientation.txt, llms-structural-tension-charts.txt, llms-structural-thinking.gemini.txt, llms-narrative-beats.txt, llms-digital-decision-making.md, llms-managerial-moment-of-truth.md, llms-kinship-hub-system.md, llms-inquiry-6406eb37-69b1-471d-9cac-07ae69449c35.md and recently something that should help me in my academic positioning which is implied in counter_articles/

I develop 2 systems (as far as I know):
1. "Relational Science" - decolonizing European/Western Culture Biased approaches
2. "Ceremonial Technology Development" - making the relationship with knowledge not just problem-solving but a different orientation

Both decolonize anything that is "European/Western Culture Biased" and try to make the relationship with knowledge not just problem-solving but a different orientation.

I think that you should cover in the ./docs all aspects of what I am doing. You can probably find many of what is coming next in llms-inquiry-medicine-wheel-abundance-intelligence-research--d971fc13-64ee-41ab-8fcd-bb5900197ab8.md that I had just drafted, you could fetch all the implied packages in there, use the llms-txt-compliance-checklist.md to draft llms-medicine-wheel-packages.txt and link them into the llms.txt and llms-full.txt in the process of what you will create in ./docs.

All the referenced LLMS-txt in this message should be the primary focus to help in the work.

The ./docs can contain as many markdown files linked together and pointing to relevant llms-txt or other files (using ../) and should support me in what you understood I am doing.

Key deliverables:
- Minimal README.md pointing to ./docs
- Comprehensive ./docs/ directory with interlinked markdown documentation
- llms-medicine-wheel-packages.txt (using compliance checklist format)
- Updated llms.txt and llms-full.txt with new references
- Documentation covering: Relational Science, Ceremonial Technology Development, Creative Orientation, Structural Tension, RISE Framework, Narrative Craft, Digital Decision Making, Kinship Hub System, Counter Articles/Academic Positioning, Medicine Wheel/Abundance Intelligence research
- All docs should make the portfolio credible for Context7 verification

primary.target: comprehensive ./docs documentation hub for Context7 verification credibility

secondary.target: README.md to point to ./docs as primary documentation
llms.txt and llms-full.txt with new references
Ceremonial Technology Development system across docs
site for Context7 verification at https://llms.jgwill.com/
secondary.dependency: all docs and llms-txt updates complete

secondary.target: .github/skills/ to corresponding llms-txt files for future evolution
docs with ../llms-*.txt references using relative paths
framing away from problem-solving toward creative orientation throughout docs

assumptions: ./docs directory probably does not exist yet
GitHub Pages publishes from ./ root, not ./docs
Current README.md is the landing page for GitHub Pages site
Context7 will fetch and visit https://llms.jgwill.com/ to verify credibility
The current site content is outdated
.github/skills/ contains skills that relate to the llms-txt files
The medicine wheel inquiry doc contains references to packages that need extraction
Robert Fritz's structural tension work is a core framework
Indigenous Knowledge Stewardship License governs knowledge in this repo
Two primary systems are being developed: Relational Science and Ceremonial Technology Development
Counter articles serve academic positioning purposes

communications: Context7 verification application with credible site

east.text: Envision the portfolio as a living interdisciplinary knowledge system at the nexus of Indigenous epistemologies, AI systems, and narrative storytelling
Recognize two core systems: Relational Science and Ceremonial Technology Development
Understand that credibility for Context7 verification requires navigable, discoverable documentation
See the decolonial orientation as foundational—not problem-solving but creative orientation

south.text: Read and analyze all referenced llms-txt files to extract key concepts, frameworks, and relationships
Analyze llms-inquiry-medicine-wheel doc for implied packages
Study llms-txt-compliance-checklist.md for proper formatting of new llms-txt files
Inventory counter_articles/ for academic positioning content
Understand the Robert Fritz structural tension methodology as it threads through multiple files

west.text: Validate that all docs interlink correctly using relative paths
Ensure llms-medicine-wheel-packages.txt follows compliance checklist format
Verify llms.txt and llms-full.txt are properly updated
Confirm README.md is minimal and properly directs to ./docs
Assess that the site would be credible to a Context7 reviewer visiting https://llms.jgwill.com/

north.text: Create ./docs/ directory structure with comprehensive interlinked markdown files
Update README.md to be minimal, pointing to ./docs
Update llms.txt and llms-full.txt with new file references

actionStack.text: Read and analyze all referenced llms-txt files
Draft llms-medicine-wheel-packages.txt using compliance checklist format
actionStack.dependency: medicine wheel analysis and compliance checklist study

actionStack.text: Update README.md to be minimal, pointing to ./docs
Validate all interlinks and relative paths

ambiguities.text: Exact structure of docs/index.md - should it mirror a navigation sidebar or be a narrative overview?
ambiguities.suggestion: Create as both: narrative overview with clear navigation sections

ambiguities.text: How deeply to document Robert Fritz's work vs. the Indigenous epistemological frameworks
ambiguities.suggestion: Present Fritz's work as methodology within the larger decolonial framework

ambiguities.text: Whether .github/skills/ mapping is part of this immediate deliverable or future work
ambiguities.suggestion: Document the relationship in docs/skills-and-companions.md but don't restructure skills/

ambiguities.text: How the GitHub Pages site renders - is there a Jekyll config or is it raw markdown?
ambiguities.suggestion: Check for _config.yml or similar; assume raw markdown served via GitHub Pages

ambiguities.text: Which specific packages are implied in the medicine wheel inquiry doc
ambiguities.suggestion: Read the file and extract all referenced packages/tools/systems

ambiguities.text: Whether 'Relational Science' and 'Ceremonial Technology Development' are formal named systems or emergent descriptions
ambiguities.suggestion: Treat as formal systems with the caveat that the user acknowledges they may discover more
